steps.title: Identify the grammatical elements
steps.content: “Something new” is a noun phrase. “Sequencing the DNA found in Neanderthals' fossilized dental plaque” is a gerund phrase.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration or appositive that clarifies a noun phrase.

The correct answer uses a colon to introduce “sequencing the DNA found in...” as an elaboration of “something new.” The distractors use a semicolon where the second part is not an independent clause, place the colon awkwardly after “sequencing,” or create a sentence fragment with a period.

That is the correct answer.
